summary: KP current state: RFC-0018 (accepted 2026-07-02) + ADR-0029 (accepted, amended same day) define a personal, self-hosted, local-first, git-native system with a sacred canonical/derived split (Zotero + a NEW Obsidian vault canonical; every index/model derived+disposable), four planes (ingestion/index/access/librarian), five build phases (spine→ingestion→index→maintenance→discovery), and placement on the real 3-host fleet: MCP servers (Zotero MCP, vault/retrieval MCP, ingress/TLS) on pve-net "G11" (always-on tier, I/O-bound only); OCR/embedding/vector+graph indices/librarian agent VM on pve (M5 Ultra, "rebooted freely"); pve-ha excluded. Zotero: zotero.org sync keeps metadata, attachments move to self-hosted WebDAV on CT 206 (192.0.2.15, staged pct-create command still awaiting Alex's approval — the only unrun action). Phase 1 partially done: alex/knowledge-plane monorepo scaffolded and pushed to git-forge (single commit b5a8c36) with packages/{ingest,index,mcp-retrieval,librarian,shared}, fleet/compose.yml, vault/ (intentionally empty), now.md, .mcp.json (all TODOs), and a "locked" frontmatter schema. Critically, the scaffold predates the MCP-on-G11 amendment — its .mcp.json, compose.yml, and README all still say "MCP servers run client-side on Alex's Mac" and "Obsidian MCP needs the live desktop app," both superseded. The 2026-07-03 expansion (KP = primary media surface; Curio as the native reading surface, core-plus-heads architecture with frontmatter-v1 + events-v1 contracts) is captured only in an inbox idea note + the Curio plan; the RFC-0018 amendment for it explicitly waits on the spine. Digest delivery: RFC-0018's "push-notifier digest delivery" must be re-read through ADR-0028 (accepted, governing) — push-notifier is KEPT but LAN-only/pull-based (upstream-base-url stripped, no off-LAN or background push exists that satisfies the fully-local rule; Pushover/ADR-0027 superseded before implementation, Telegram from the brief is dead). So the digest's primary artifact must be the digest note itself (proposals/digest-{date}.md in the vault — durable, git-native, and consumable by Curio as a reading-surface item), with push-notifier as an optional LAN-only 1-line ping in Alex's instance and a pluggable notifier (webhook/push-notifier/none) in the OSS system. Two retrieval legs silently lost their implementation when the live-Obsidian-app dependency was dropped: BM25 (RFC still says "free via Obsidian REST plugin/Omnisearch") and the graph store question (brief's Kùzu is embedded/file-based — same cross-host problem ADR-0029 flagged for LanceDB, but the RFC only re-litigated the vector store). Nothing in the accepted docs addresses Alex's new distributability requirement — the design assumes his exact stack (llm-gateway/llm-observability/managed-postgres/git-forge/G11 topology) throughout, and the KP frontmatter schema conflicts field-by-field with Curio's published frontmatter-v1 (source-as-enum vs source-as-URL, authors vs author, date_added vs saved, no curio_id/schema_version), so "near-drop-in" is overstated and a deliberate schema reconciliation (KP schema doc's own rule: a field change is a migration) is the first native-integration design task.
facts: RFC-0018 (rfc/0018-knowledge-plane.md, status: accepted, 2026-07-02): canonical = Zotero (PDFs, annotations, bib metadata, DOIs, item relations) + a NEW Obsidian vault (separate from the management-plane vault; markdown+YAML, git-versioned); everything else (vector index, link/citation/claim graph, full-text index, interest model) is derived and rebuildable; agents touch the vault only via reviewable proposal branches, never direct edits to evergreens.

RFC-0018 four planes: (1) Ingestion — RSS/web clips/Zotero PDFs into vault inbox/ as normalized markdown with strict frontmatter; OCR routing PyMuPDF4LLM (native-text) / Docling (academic/tables, default) / Marker (messy scans), all CPU-only on this fleet so OCR is batch; (2) Index — vector (Ollama local embeddings: bge-m3/nomic-embed-text/qwen3-embedding), link+citation+claim graph with supports/refutes edges, BM25; retrieval fuses BM25+vector+one-hop graph; incremental reindex keyed on frontmatter checksum; (3) Access — Zotero MCP, vault MCP, custom retrieval MCP (search_hybrid, find_contradicting, unlinked_neighbors); (4) Librarian — maintenance loop (frequent, drains inbox, writes only to proposals/ branch) + discovery loop (digest cadence, reads now.md, interest model, emits ONE digest).

ADR-0029 placement (accepted, amended 2026-07-02 per Alex 'mcp should be on g11'): MCP server processes themselves + ingress/TLS run ON pve-net (G11), not proxied-from-elsewhere; Zotero MCP reads a WebDAV-synced local copy of attachments; vault/retrieval MCP reads a git-mirrored vault copy off disk (drops the live-Obsidian-desktop-app dependency entirely); OCR/embedding/vector+graph indices/librarian VM on pve (M5 Ultra); pve-ha excluded (~3 GB free, RFC-0012 decoupling).

ADR-0029 constraint: pve-net's embedded cores (Ryzen R2514 4C/8T, ~11.6 GiB usable, ~2 cores/4-5 GB genuinely free per inbox/knowledge-plane-hardware-placement.md) stay reserved for network I/O and light polling — no OCR or embedding workload ever lands there; MCP + WebDAV are I/O-bound and fit.

pve (M5 Ultra, Ryzen 7 7730U 8C/16T, ~28 GiB usable, no GPU) is already oversubscribed on nominal allocation even with win11 stopped; ADR-0029 requires measuring real live utilization (pvesh get / free -h) before Phase 3 commits embedding workload; fallback if pve can't absorb it = scale down librarian cadence/scope, not new hardware (infra/pve.md, adr/0029).

Zotero sync resolved 2026-07-02 (Alex): zotero.org's own sync stays for metadata (items/collections/tags/notes — server-side at api.zotero.org); ONLY file attachments (PDFs) move to self-hosted WebDAV. No headless Zotero.app anywhere. The provisioning task's analysis says the Zotero MCP should prefer the Zotero Web API (his account, an API key) for metadata + read PDF bytes off the co-located WebDAV storage dir (tasks/provision-zotero-webdav-g11.md).

CT 206 (hostname zotero, unprivileged Debian 13 LXC, 1 core/1024 MB/16 GB rootfs, 192.0.2.15/22, gw 192.0.2.1, nameserver 192.0.2.69, searchdomain example.internal) is STAGED AND NOT YET CREATED — awaiting Alex's per-action approval; it is the single open server-side action (tasks/provision-zotero-webdav-g11.md status: planned; inbox/knowledge-plane-handoff-2026-07-02.html §1; no log entry since shows it run).

Provisioning plan for CT 206: nginx + ngx_http_dav_module/dav_ext (nginx-extras — the combo Zotero's docs list as tested-compatible), basic-auth .htpasswd escrowed to secrets-manager; IaC role ansible/roles/zotero_webdav deliberately deferred until hand-proven (untested-role risk); backups per ADR-0010 (daily local + weekly net-offnode) once the role lands; pve-net CT DNS gotcha: must set nameserver at create or host's overlay-vpn MagicDNS resolv.conf breaks DNS.

Phase 1 scaffold state (clone at scratchpad/knowledge-plane, = origin/main, single commit b5a8c36): package.json = bare npm-workspaces root (name knowledge-plane, private, workspaces packages/*); all five packages are README-only stubs, zero code; .gitignore excludes packages/index/data/, *.lance/, *.kuzu/; vault/ intentionally empty pending Alex's manual migration; now.md has one example thread (LTC vs transformers); repo home http://192.0.2.2:3000/alex/knowledge-plane (git-forge).

Locked KP frontmatter schema (packages/shared/frontmatter-schema.md): title, source (enum zotero|rss|web|manual — drives OCR routing), zotero_key (round-trip handle, required iff source: zotero), url, authors (array), doi, date_added, tags, status (unprocessed→linked→promoted — the maintenance loop's queue field), checksum ('sha256:…' — the reindex/dedupe key). Explicit rule: 'a field added here is a migration, not a drive-by edit.' Non-goal: claim graph edges stay in the derived graph index, never in frontmatter.

Curio's published contracts (wf-results/designs.json core-plus-heads winner, roadmap.json Phase 3): frontmatter-v1 = title, author, source URL, feed, published, saved, tags, curio_id (UUIDv7), checksum (sha256 of normalized body), schema_version — rendered via minijinja templates; events-v1 = JSONL {schema_version, event: subscribed|read|starred|read_later|promoted, article{curio_id,url,title,feed}, ts} with file sink (daily rotation + cursor checkpointing, pointable at the vault's .curio/events/) and webhook sink; named-destination registry (no raw paths cross IPC/CLI); in-vault .curio/manifest.json (curio_id → vault-relative path + last checksum) enables wipe-and-reinstall reconciliation; atomic tmp+rename writes because the vault is git-/watcher-observed; both schemas published as semver'd JSON Schema artifacts with a written breaking-change policy and a MANDATORY schema-review gate before the first external consumer wires in — 'the KP becomes consumer #1 immediately.'

Curio design intent: KP integration is configuration only (point vault export at the KP vault's inbox/, point the librarian's interest model at the JSONL sink), zero KP-specific code in Curio; Curio's SQLite is derived/disposable; future annotations/highlights are specified to land in the vault as file edits, never the app DB; v1 ships NO sync and NO server (honest single-machine desktop + CLI); future seams = curio-server head (GReader/Fever APIs, G11 could front it) and client mode (StateBackend port over FreshRSS/Miniflux/Feedbin) (designs.json kp_seam + sync_story).

Curio status (tasks/curio-production-plan.md, status: review, owner: alex): core-plus-heads won 3-0; ~28-week 6-phase roadmap; Phase 3 (3.5 weeks) = vault export + event log + curio-cli; awaiting Alex's full plan review + identity decisions (name/domain, Apple Developer, Windows signing); Plan Addendum from Alex's 'looking good' review: curio repo must ship CLAUDE.md + .claude/skills/, justfile front door, enforced-and-honest coverage ratchet (tight-DX doctrine).

Digest-delivery governing decision is ADR-0028 (accepted 2026-06-30): alerting stays push-notifier, LAN-only, pull-based; upstream-base-url stripped so literally nothing leaves the LAN; NO fully-local iOS background push exists (APNs is cloud-only); delivery = opening the push-notifier app or the status.example.internal Gatus page; ADR-0027 (Pushover) superseded before implementation (never applied); tasks/decommission-push-notifier-g11.md declined — push-notifier CT 203 (192.0.2.76, push-notifier.example.internal via Caddy, auth deny-all, publisher token in secrets-manager) is kept as the LAN alert bus.

Therefore the KP digest mechanism consistent with house decisions: primary artifact = the digest note itself (proposals/digest-{date}.md committed to the vault — git-native, durable, readable in Obsidian/Curio); push-notifier = optional LAN-only pull ping in Alex's instance; anything push/cloud (Telegram from the brief, Pushover) is forbidden by ADR-0028's fully-local stance; the OSS system needs a pluggable notifier (webhook/push-notifier/file/none) — Curio's events webhook sink and the digest-as-feed-item are the native candidates.

Reuse targets, verified: llm-gateway CT 106 (192.0.2.5, http://llm.example.internal) = OpenAI-compatible gateway fronting Anthropic/OpenAI/Gemini/OpenRouter/MiniMax/z.ai with provider wildcards + curated aliases, virtual keys, Redis response cache, Prometheus /metrics, llm-observability_otel callback (cost-emitting patch pinned to llm-gateway 1.88.1); llm-observability CT 107 (192.0.2.6, llm-observability.example.internal) = llm-observability v3, per-agent projects (pi-agent, claude-agent) — a librarian project would follow that pattern; managed-postgres CT 111 (192.0.2.9, managed-postgres.example.internal) = 11-service self-hosted managed-postgres, Postgres pinned 15.8.1.085 (PG15; PG17 migration one-way, not yet done), schema-per-app tenancy via managed-postgres-schema-add.sh, direct Postgres via Supavisor 192.0.2.9:5432 (session)/:6543 (transaction); pgvector presence in the image is still UNCONFIRMED (RFC-0018 explicitly says 'worth confirming').

Vector-store lean (RFC-0018 open question, deferred to Phase 3): pgvector over LanceDB, because ADR-0029 put the MCP query layer (G11) on a different host than the index builder (pve) — a network-native store avoids NFS-sharing a LanceDB file; managed-postgres Postgres is already a network service.

Repo boundary (RFC-0018 §Repo boundary, RFC-0014/ADR-0022): KP code lives in alex/knowledge-plane, never in the management-plane vault; the vault only vendors code back as a pinned submodule under ansible/roles/**/files/ if a piece runs as a managed hypervisor guest; repo creation was Alex's manual git-forge action (IaC token has no repo-create rights).

Phase status: Phase 1 (spine) partially done — repo + scaffold + locked schema done; NOT done: vault/ populated (Alex's manual migration, his timeline — existing Obsidian vault is in a personal GitHub repo, Zotero local-only on his Mac), Zotero MCP install/connect/read-test, vault/retrieval MCP (blocked on vault migration — 'nothing to serve yet'). Phases 2-5 not started (README.md, tasks/knowledge-plane.md acceptance criteria).

2026-07-03 expansion (inbox/knowledge-plane-primary-media-surface.md): Alex wants the KP as his PRIMARY media/reading/reference surface — adds (1) a consumption surface whose highlights/annotations flow back to canonical stores, (2) wider funnel (web articles, newsletters, podcasts/video via batch whisper.cpp on the CPU-only fleet, EPUB), (3) a behavioral interest model learning from read/annotate/finish/promote signals, not just now.md. Constraint recorded: do NOT disturb the build order; the KP-integration RFC-0018 amendment waits on the spine (Phase 1-2); reading-surface candidates (Karakeep/Readeck/Wallabag) resolved in favor of building Curio.

Zotero MCP package pick is explicitly open and is the provisioning task's first real step: RFC-0018 names 54yyyu/zotero-mcp-server + zotero-cli and ZotPilot as candidates, but both are described as sqlite-reading; the task's analysis prefers a Web-API-native tool (Zotero Web API for metadata + local WebDAV dir for PDF bytes) (tasks/provision-zotero-webdav-g11.md).

House DX doctrine to replicate in KP repos (CLAUDE.md, curio Plan Addendum, memory/prefer-deterministic-tooling): every repo ships git hooks, CI gates, a justfile, and a .claude/ agent layer (CLAUDE.md + skills); deterministic gates over agentic 'remember to run X'; incidents close with machinery (gate/tripwire/runbook), not just fixes.

Fleet environmental risk: the G11 router-fw router cutover was scheduled ~2026-07-01 (memory/g11-router-cutover) but no log entry on 07-02/07-03 records it happening; pve-net's infra note (synced 06-29) still says 'Not yet routing'; the staged CT 206 command assumes gw=192.0.2.1 and pve-net guests 200/202/203/204/205 exist (router-fw, pihole, push-notifier, gatus, caddy) — CT 206 provisioning timing interacts with the cutover window.

The KP brief's librarian starter prompt (brief §C): hard limits — never edit outside proposals/, never assert an unsourced claim, emit ONE digest or say nothing cleared threshold, surface the strongest contradiction; then push proposals/ to a worktree branch + push-notifier a 1-line summary. The interest model is spec'd as explicit topic/claim vectors with evidence weights and posterior relevance ('Fugue-as-inference over the claim graph'), not naive cosine top-k.

The brief's named production risks (§05, all still open decisions): write conflicts (section-scoped patches + proposal branches + git merge), annotation round-trip fidelity (decide early: annotation canonical in Zotero mirrored read-only into vault, vs promoted into a note that owns it), incremental reindex, OCR quality gate (low-confidence pages to a review queue, never silently into the claim graph), embedding lock-in (version the index against one model; swap = deliberate migration), notification discipline (threshold + digest batching).

Existing agent-VM pattern the librarian clones: claude-agent VM 110 + pi-agent VM 109 on pve (Claude Code on cron, Docker), plus RFC-0013 per-project NFS workspaces (projects-ctl on host is the single mutation primitive, proj mounts on agent VMs) for the proposals/ worktree (adr/0029, CLAUDE.md, hardware-placement doc).
open_questions: CT 206 create: still awaiting Alex's explicit approval — and should its network params/timing be re-checked against the (possibly imminent or already-attempted) G11 router cutover (gw 192.0.2.1, DHCP/DNS topology)?

Zotero MCP package pick: which Web-API-native server (vs the sqlite-reading candidates RFC-0018 names)? Where does the Zotero API key live (secrets-manager escrow per house discipline)? Does any candidate read PDFs from a WebDAV-storage-format dir (Zotero stores .zip'd attachments on WebDAV — does the MCP need to unzip)?

LanceDB vs managed-postgres pgvector (leaning pgvector): is pgvector actually present in the pinned managed-postgres PG15 image? Does KP-as-schema-in-managed-postgres couple a derived index to a canonical-adjacent shared service, and what does the OSS system use instead (any Postgres+pgvector via a storage interface)? Same question was never asked for the GRAPH store — brief says Kùzu (embedded, file-based), which has the identical cross-host problem now that mcp-retrieval is on G11.

BM25 replacement: RFC-0018 still sources full-text/BM25 from the Obsidian REST plugin/Omnisearch, which requires the live desktop app the G11 amendment dropped — what implements the BM25 leg now (tantivy, SQLite FTS5, Postgres FTS co-located with pgvector)?

Vault mirror mechanics on G11 (entirely unspecified): where is the canonical vault repo (git-forge? still personal GitHub?), how does the G11 mirror update (cron pull, push hook), how do librarian proposal branches flow (push from pve VM to the remote, mirror never writes?), and what serves the vault MCP surface over the mirrored files (custom server? which existing package)?

Frontmatter schema reconciliation — the first native-Curio design task: KP schema vs curio frontmatter-v1 conflict on source (origin-enum vs URL), authors vs author, date_added vs saved, checksum format ('sha256:…' prefix vs bare sha256-of-normalized-body), and KP lacks curio_id + schema_version. Who owns the superset schema, does KP adopt schema_version, and does 'source: rss' notes' OCR-routing semantics survive? (KP's own doc: a field change is a migration.)

Ingestion-plane ownership split: with Curio as the designated reading surface, does packages/ingest still own RSS fetching (the brief's FreshRSS/Miniflux recommendation looks dead)? Curio is a desktop app that isn't always running — does unattended feed ingestion need a server-side path (future curio-server head on G11? curio-cli on cron? a thin KP fetcher for non-Curio sources only: Zotero PDFs, newsletters, transcripts)?

Events-v1 transport for the interest model: file sink into the vault's .curio/events/ synced via git (personal behavioral data in a git repo — acceptable for Alex's private vault, but the OSS story?) vs webhook sink to a G11 endpoint? Cursor semantics if both?

Digest delivery final spec: is digest-note-in-vault (primary) + optional LAN push-notifier ping + digest-as-Curio-feed-item the accepted mechanism, and what's the pluggable notifier abstraction for the OSS system?

OSS distribution architecture (new directive, nowhere in accepted docs): what splits 'the system' (open-source, replicable, pluggable LLM gateway / vector store / git remote / notifier; Obsidian recommended viewer never a dependency) from 'Alex's instance' (llm-gateway, llm-observability, managed-postgres, git-forge, G11/pve placement, his vault + now.md)? Does the current monorepo shape survive that split (vault/ and now.md are instance data inside the system repo)?

Where does the OSS KP repo live publicly (Curio is on GitHub; KP is on private git-forge — RFC-0016 GitHub-mirroring machinery exists), and under what license (Curio Phase-3 artifacts imply schema/license decisions; Marker is GPL-3.0 — does the OCR router's optional Marker path constrain licensing)?
now.md placement: it is librarian input (arguably canonical, hand-authored) but lives in the code monorepo — move to the vault?
vault/ mechanics: git submodule pointing at the vault repo vs contents-in-tree (vault/README leaves both open); interacts with the OSS system/instance split and with the G11 mirror design.

Annotation round-trip (brief risk #2, undecided): Zotero-canonical annotations mirrored read-only into the vault, or promotion transfers ownership to the note? Same question now extends to Curio highlights (spec says vault file edits — which files, what schema?).

Behavioral interest model: which events-v1 signals feed it, how are implicit signals weighted vs explicit now.md threads, and does any of this write back to canonical stores (it must not — interest model is derived)?

pve real utilization: still unmeasured (pvesh get / free -h) — required before Phase 3 commits OCR/embedding/Ollama workload; also whether Ollama-on-CPU embedding throughput is acceptable or the embedder should ride llm-gateway to a local/remote model in Alex's instance (OSS: pluggable embedder).
Vault migration timing: Alex's manual move of the GitHub Obsidian vault + local Zotero library — unblocks the vault/retrieval MCP CT and Phases 2+; no date.

Curio schema-review gate: KP planning must schedule the mandatory frontmatter-v1/events-v1 review before wiring in as consumer #1 — who reviews, against what KP acceptance test (the roadmap's 'generic-consumer proof' script run against a KP-shaped inbox/)?
stale_or_wrong: Scaffold .mcp.json is stale on its face: its $comment says both MCP servers 'run client-side on Alex's Mac today' and the obsidian entry requires coddingtonbear's Local REST API with the live desktop app — superseded hours later by the ADR-0029 amendment (both MCPs on G11; vault MCP reads a git mirror; no live app). Its zotero comment also says 'reads zotero.sqlite directly' — superseded by the Web-API-first analysis in tasks/provision-zotero-webdav-g11.md.

Scaffold fleet/compose.yml is stale twice: 'in Phase 1 they run client-side on Alex's Mac' (superseded by G11) and 'this file gains real services starting Phase 3, when mcp-retrieval is deployed on pve' — ADR-0029 puts the MCP server processes on G11, not pve (only the indices it queries live on pve).
Scaffold README.md Phase-1 checklist repeats the superseded constraint: 'Obsidian MCP server (needs the live desktop app, not headless)' — dropped by the git-mirrored-copy design.

RFC-0018's placement table entry 'push-notifier digest delivery' is underdesigned given ADR-0028 (which predates the RFC): push-notifier exists but is LAN-only pull-based with no background/off-LAN push — a digest 'delivered' via push-notifier is only seen when Alex opens the app on the LAN. The digest mechanism needs respecifying as digest-note-in-vault primary + optional LAN ping + pluggable notifier for OSS. The design brief's header 'Notify: push-notifier · Telegram' is flatly wrong under ADR-0028's fully-local rule (Telegram = cloud), as is any Pushover path (ADR-0027 superseded, never applied).

RFC-0018 §Index still claims full-text/BM25 is 'free via the Obsidian REST plugin/Omnisearch' — that implementation died with the live-desktop-app dependency the same RFC's placement section removed; the BM25 leg of the fused retrieval currently has no implementation.

The brief's graph-store pick (Kùzu, embedded/file-based) was never re-examined after the MCP layer moved cross-host — ADR-0029 re-litigated only the vector store (LanceDB→pgvector lean); an embedded graph file on pve is unreachable from the retrieval MCP on G11 by the same argument.

RFC-0018 §Alternatives 'mature MCP options exist, only retrieval-fusion is custom' is now overstated: the named Obsidian options (coddingtonbear built-in, cyanheads) both ride the Local REST API plugin requiring the live app, and the named Zotero options read zotero.sqlite — under the amended design (git-mirror vault MCP, Web-API Zotero MCP) more of the access plane is custom or needs different packages than the RFC records.

The brief's ingestion-plane recommendation to self-host FreshRSS or Miniflux as the feed reader is effectively superseded by the 2026-07-03 Curio decision (Curio is the designated RSS/reading surface with its own fetch/normalize/export pipeline); packages/ingest's README still describes RSS fetchers as its Phase-2 scope — the Curio/ingest ownership split is undesigned.

The Curio design's claim that its frontmatter-v1 is 'a near-drop-in for the KP inbox note contract' overstates: field-level conflicts with the locked KP schema (source enum vs source URL, authors vs author, date_added vs saved, missing curio_id/schema_version in KP, checksum format prefix) mean reconciliation is a real schema migration, which the KP's own schema doc requires treating deliberately.

Nothing in RFC-0018/ADR-0029/the scaffold anticipates the distributability requirement: the design hard-codes Alex's instance choices (llm-gateway+llm-observability for LLM calls, managed-postgres pgvector lean, git-forge remote, G11/pve host names in docs and comments, .mcp.json shape) with no pluggability seams, no license, no system/instance boundary — the monorepo even contains instance data (vault/, now.md).

RFC-0018's librarian placement note 'emits one digest via push-notifier' in packages/librarian/README.md carries the same ADR-0028 qualification gap (pull-only) and is instance-specific (OSS needs a notifier abstraction).

tasks/knowledge-plane.md Status Log says the remaining CT-206 step is 'a live approval in-conversation, not a queued Home action' with owner: alex removed — but the approval has now been pending across sessions (staged 2026-07-02, still unrun 2026-07-03); per the house 'waiting on Alex' rule this arguably should be owner: alex in the Home queue.

The 2026-07-03 primary-media-surface expansion (consumption surface, wider funnel incl. whisper.cpp transcription/newsletters/EPUB, behavioral interest model) exists only as an inbox idea note — RFC-0018 has not been amended; the accepted RFC's scope (research-assistant only) understates the now-directed system, and the promised amendment is explicitly parked until Phase 1-2 prove the spine.

Hardware-placement doc and ADR-0029 headroom numbers for pve-net predate CT 206 and any future vault/retrieval-MCP CT and the router cutover (router-fw VM 200 taking both physical NICs + 2c/4GB when live) — G11's '~2 cores / 4-5 GB free' needs re-derivation before adding more KP guests there.
